text: A straightforward iron catalysis approach to 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkynes and alkenes was developed. Reaction with iron catalyst FeBr2 and base Cs2CO3 at room temperature gives title products of interest for further cross coupling reactions in high yields without any compatibility issue. A detailed catalyst search revealed that transition metals could catalyze the reaction, however iron catalysts were the most efficient for this reaction. The obtained iodinated products were subsequently cross-coupled using Suzuki, Sonogashira or iron catalysis Grignard reactions, giving highly fluorinated molecules.
